zh.lifeSummary: 當Johanna Gifford於1346年出生在英國蘇格蘭易亞斯特 洛迪安也斯特吉福德時，她的父親Sir Hugh Giffard of Yester, Knight24歲，母親Lady Joanna Douglas, Lady Yester32歲。她於1372年與Sir Thomas Hay of Locherworth在英國蘇格蘭度福利爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1400年1月1日享年54歲。

nameMeanings.name: Gifford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Giffard, either from the Middle English personal name Gifard, derived from an Old French name of this form deriving from ancient Germanic geb- ‘give, gift’ + hard- ‘hardy, brave’, or perhaps sometimes a nickname from Old French gif(f)ard ‘chubby-cheeked, bloated’.

variants.name: Jeffords
meanings: English: variant of Gifford , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Johanna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Johanna Gifford was born in 1346, in Gifford, Yester, East Lothian, Scotland, United Kingdom, her father, Sir Hugh Giffard of Yester, Knight, was 24 and her mother, Lady Joanna Douglas, Lady Yester, was 32. She married Sir Thomas Hay of Locherworth in 1372, in Dumfriesshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 1 January 1400, at the age of 54.

Elle a épousé Sir Thomas Hay of Locherworth en 1372, à Dumfriesshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 1 janvier 1400, à l’âge de 54 ans.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Johanna Gifford родилась в 1346 г. в местности гиффорд, иестир, эаст лотян, Шотландия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir Hugh Giffard of Yester, Knight было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Joanna Douglas, Lady Yester, 32. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas Hay of Locherworth в 1372 г. в местности думфриессхир, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 1 января 1400 г. в возрасте 54 лет.
